learning_objectives: Map the digital marketing ecosystem: owned, earned, and paid media
Develop a digital marketing strategy aligned with business objectives
Understand the digital customer journey and touchpoints

lecture_notes.introduction: Digital marketing encompasses all marketing efforts that use electronic devices or the internet. It has fundamentally changed how businesses reach customers and how customers discover, evaluate, and purchase products.
key_concepts: Digital marketing channels: search, social, email, content, display, affiliate
Owned media (website, blog), earned media (shares, reviews), paid media (ads)
Digital customer journey: awareness, consideration, conversion, retention, advocacy
RACE framework: Reach, Act, Convert, Engage
Digital marketing strategy alignment with business KPIs
lecture_notes.demonstration: Auditing a brand's digital presence across all channels and identifying gaps and opportunities
setup_requirements: Textbook: Chaffey & Ellis-Chadwick, Digital Marketing (8th ed.)

review.value: Reach (build awareness), Act (encourage interaction), Convert (drive sales), Engage (build loyalty and advocacy)
Non-linear path with multiple touchpoints across devices and channels; attribution is a key challenge

class_schedule.topic: Search Engine Optimization and Content Marketing
learning_objectives: Apply on-page and off-page SEO techniques
Develop a content marketing strategy that supports SEO goals
Use keyword research tools to identify content opportunities

lecture_notes.introduction: SEO and content marketing work together: great content attracts links and engagement that improve search rankings, while SEO ensures that content reaches the right audience through organic search.
key_concepts: On-page SEO: title tags, meta descriptions, headers, internal linking
Off-page SEO: backlinks, domain authority, social signals
Technical SEO: site speed, mobile-friendliness, structured data
Content strategy: topic clusters, pillar pages, content calendar
Keyword research: search volume, difficulty, intent mapping
lecture_notes.demonstration: Live SEO audit of a real website using Google Search Console and keyword research with Ahrefs or SEMrush
setup_requirements: Keyword research tool access (SEMrush or Ahrefs)

review.value: Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, Trustworthiness: Google's quality signals for ranking content
A pillar page covering a broad topic linked to cluster content pages covering specific subtopics; demonstrates topical authority to search engines

class_schedule.topic: Social Media Marketing and Influencer Strategy
learning_objectives: Evaluate influencer marketing partnerships for ROI
Create a social media content calendar and engagement plan

lecture_notes.introduction: Social media platforms are where audiences spend their time, but each platform has different demographics, content formats, and algorithms. Effective social media marketing requires platform-native strategies.
key_concepts: Platform strategy: Instagram (visual), LinkedIn (B2B), TikTok (short video), X/Twitter (real-time)
Organic reach decline and algorithm-driven distribution
Influencer tiers: nano, micro, macro, mega; ROI differences
Community management and social listening
Social media metrics: engagement rate, reach, conversions, share of voice
lecture_notes.demonstration: Designing a 30-day social media campaign for a product launch, including content calendar, influencer outreach, and KPIs

review.value: (Likes + Comments + Shares) / Followers x 100; micro-influencers typically have higher engagement rates than mega-influencers
Monitoring brand mentions, competitor mentions, and industry conversations to inform strategy and respond to sentiment

class_schedule.topic: Paid Digital Advertising: Search and Display
learning_objectives: Set up and optimize Google Ads search campaigns
Understand programmatic advertising and display networks
Calculate and optimize key advertising metrics: CPC, CTR, ROAS

lecture_notes.introduction: Paid advertising provides immediate visibility and precise targeting. Understanding the auction mechanisms, bidding strategies, and optimization techniques is essential for efficient ad spend.
key_concepts: Google Ads: keyword targeting, Quality Score, ad rank
Ad auction: CPC = (Ad Rank below / Your Quality Score) + $0.01
Display advertising: GDN, programmatic buying, RTB
Retargeting/remarketing: reaching previous visitors
Ad metrics: impressions, clicks, CTR, CPC, conversions, ROAS
lecture_notes.demonstration: Setting up a Google Ads search campaign: keyword research, ad copy writing, bid strategy selection, and conversion tracking

review.value: Google's rating of keyword relevance, ad quality, and landing page experience; higher Quality Score = lower CPC and better ad position
Return on Ad Spend = Revenue from ads / Cost of ads; the primary measure of advertising efficiency

learning_objectives: Design email marketing campaigns that drive conversions
Build automated marketing funnels with trigger-based workflows
Measure email performance and optimize through A/B testing

lecture_notes.introduction: Email remains one of the highest-ROI digital marketing channels. Combined with marketing automation, it enables personalized, timely communication at scale.
key_concepts: List building: opt-in strategies, lead magnets, segmentation
Email types: welcome series, newsletters, promotional, transactional
Marketing automation workflows: triggers, conditions, actions
Personalization and dynamic content
Email metrics: open rate, CTR, conversion rate, unsubscribe rate
lecture_notes.demonstration: Building a complete automated welcome series in Mailchimp: 5 emails over 14 days with branching logic based on engagement

review.value: Software that automates repetitive marketing tasks based on triggers and rules, enabling personalized communication at scale
Getting emails into the inbox (not spam); depends on sender reputation, authentication (SPF, DKIM, DMARC), and engagement metrics

class_schedule.topic: Analytics, Attribution, and Digital Marketing Optimization
learning_objectives: Set up and interpret Google Analytics reports
Apply attribution models to evaluate channel performance
Use data-driven insights to optimize digital marketing ROI

lecture_notes.introduction: The greatest advantage of digital marketing is measurability. Analytics transforms marketing from guesswork to data-driven decision-making, but only if you measure the right things and interpret them correctly.
key_concepts: Google Analytics 4: events, conversions, user properties
Attribution models: last-click, first-click, linear, time-decay, data-driven
Conversion rate optimization: A/B testing, landing page optimization
Customer lifetime value and acquisition cost ratio
Dashboard design and marketing reporting
lecture_notes.demonstration: Building a marketing performance dashboard in Google Analytics, configuring conversion tracking, and running attribution model comparison

review.value: Rules for assigning conversion credit across touchpoints; the choice of model significantly affects how you evaluate channel performance and allocate budget
Customer Lifetime Value to Customer Acquisition Cost; healthy ratio is 3:1 or higher, meaning each customer generates 3x what it costs to acquire them

quizzes.topic: SEO, Content Marketing, and Social Media

questions.question: A B2B software company's blog gets 10,000 monthly visitors but only 50 leads. Diagnose the problem and propose three specific improvements using SEO and content marketing principles.
questions.answer: The 0.5% conversion rate suggests traffic quality or conversion path issues. Three improvements: (1) Audit keyword targeting: the blog may be attracting informational traffic with no purchase intent. Refocus content on bottom-of-funnel keywords that signal buying intent (e.g., 'best CRM for small business' vs. 'what is CRM'). (2) Add strategic CTAs and lead magnets: each blog post needs a relevant content upgrade (checklist, template, guide) gated behind an email form, aligned with the post topic. (3) Implement topic clusters: create pillar pages for each product category with supporting cluster content to build topical authority and guide visitors through the funnel from awareness to conversion.

rubric.key: Correct diagnosis of the conversion gap

questions.question: Why do micro-influencers (10K-100K followers) often deliver better marketing ROI than celebrity influencers (1M+ followers)?
questions.answer: Micro-influencers deliver better ROI for three reasons: (1) Higher engagement rates (typically 3-5% vs. under 1% for celebrities) because their audiences are more niche and personally connected. (2) Greater authenticity and trust: followers perceive micro-influencers as genuine experts in their niche rather than paid spokespeople. (3) Lower cost: the cost per engagement or cost per conversion is significantly lower, allowing brands to work with multiple micro-influencers across niches for the price of one celebrity, diversifying reach and testing different messages.

questions.question: A Google Ads campaign has a CTR of 3%, CPC of $2.50, and conversion rate of 4%. If the average order value is $75, calculate the ROAS and determine if the campaign is profitable.
questions.answer: For every 1,000 impressions: Clicks = 1,000 x 3% = 30 clicks. Cost = 30 x $2.50 = $75. Conversions = 30 x 4% = 1.2 conversions. Revenue = 1.2 x $75 = $90. ROAS = $90 / $75 = 1.2x (or 120%). The campaign generates $1.20 for every $1.00 spent. This is technically profitable but with a thin margin. Most businesses target 3x-5x ROAS to cover product costs and overhead, so this campaign needs optimization (improve conversion rate, reduce CPC, or increase average order value).

rubric.key: Identifies need for optimization with suggestions

questions.question: Explain why an email welcome series of 5 emails performs better than a single welcome email, and describe the purpose of each email in the series.
questions.answer: A welcome series builds relationship and trust incrementally rather than overwhelming the subscriber. Email 1 (immediate): Deliver the promised lead magnet and set expectations for future emails. Email 2 (day 2): Share the brand story and values to build emotional connection. Email 3 (day 4): Provide valuable educational content demonstrating expertise. Email 4 (day 7): Share social proof (testimonials, case studies) to build credibility. Email 5 (day 14): Present a specific offer or call-to-action now that trust is established. This sequence nurtures the subscriber through the funnel, and welcome series average 4x higher open rates and 5x higher click rates than regular campaigns.

rubric.key: Explains why series outperforms single email
Clear purpose for each email in series
Logical progression from trust-building to conversion

major_assignments.title: Digital Marketing Campaign Plan and Execution
major_assignments.description: Develop and partially execute a comprehensive digital marketing campaign for a real or simulated business. The campaign must integrate at least three digital channels with measurable KPIs, a content plan, a paid media plan, and an analytics framework.
objectives: Design a multi-channel digital marketing strategy
Create campaign assets: ads, emails, social content, landing pages
Set up tracking and measurement frameworks
Analyze results and optimize based on data
deliverables: Digital marketing campaign plan document (10-12 pages)
Campaign creative assets for at least three channels
Analytics dashboard design with KPI targets
Performance projection model with budget allocation

criteria: Clear target audience and customer journey mapping
Channel selection justified with audience data
Ad copy is compelling and platform-appropriate
Landing page design supports conversion goals
KPIs defined for each channel and campaign level
Visual mockups for all creative assets
Integrated multi-channel strategy with clear targeting

weekly_quizzes.description: Regular assessment of digital marketing concepts and metrics

hands_on_labs.description: Practical exercises using Google Ads, Analytics, and social media tools

major_assignments.description: Digital marketing campaign plan and execution project

midterm_examination.description: Covers SEO, content marketing, social media, and paid advertising

final_examination.description: Cumulative exam including analytics, attribution, and campaign optimization

course_policies.attendance: Regular attendance required. Hands-on lab sessions are essential and cannot be made up remotely.
course_policies.late_submissions: Campaign plan penalized 10% per day. Lab exercises must be submitted by end of lab session.
course_policies.collaboration: Campaign plan may be completed in teams of 2-3. Labs and quizzes are individual work.
course_policies.academic_integrity: Campaign assets must be original creative work. Using AI-generated content must be disclosed and is limited to ideation, not final copy.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Google Ads and Analytics certifications recommended. Access to social media management and email marketing platforms.
